sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - comparing all nodes
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

for n in before.segmentation['nodes']:
    after_n = next((an for an in after.segmentation['nodes'] if an['boundary'] == n['boundary']), None)
    if after_n is None or after_n['color'] != n['color'] or after_n['pixels'] != n['pixels']:
        print(f"Changed Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}->{after_n['color'] if after_n else 'N/A'}, pixels={n['pixels']}->{after_n['pixels'] if after_n else 'N/A'}")

# Check node counts
print(f"Before nodes: {len(before.segmentation['nodes'])}, After nodes: {len(after.segmentation['nodes'])}")

# Let me look at the top-right area more carefully - maybe there are interactive elements there
# Node 3: charcoal frame with white inside at rows 2-6, cols 35-39
# Node 4: charcoal frame with purple inside at rows 2-6, cols 41-45
# Node 8: white strip at row 7, cols 41-45
# These look like buttons or selectors

# Let me try clicking on the small white square in the top-right (Node 6)
print("\nTrying MOUSE on small white square (row 4, col 37)...")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 37}])
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Nodes: {len(seg['nodes'])}")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
